notes: Tested the analog against three hostile lenses. (1) 'This is just don't quit hard problems' — false; the verse specifies role-bound accumulated context as the operative thing, and the falsifiability handles the 'role has genuinely passed to another' case explicitly. (2) 'This will be weaponised by managers to chain engineers to bad work' — partially mitigated; the counter_example names the unjust-role case, but the engineering layer could be cited out of context. The falsifiability and counter_example together form a real defense. (3) 'No real engineer would draft an email pretending the work is fungible' — actually, this happens often with senior engineers experiencing imposter-recoil mid-migration, and the scenario captures that pattern accurately. The Postgres write-path, idempotency-key contract, fraud-shelf ordering, on-call rota are all real artifacts of a payments-platform staff role. Insight verdict: this is a real engineering distinction (role-bound context vs fungible work) that the verse captures. PASS.
